rationale: The one substantive ground-truth concern (trust_remote_code causing a regression on locked kernels<0.15.0 installs) was missed by both candidates, who instead flagged the inverse claim that trust_remote_code is absent despite the PR description mentioning it — same blind spot, so recall is equally low for both. Both are well-grounded and non-fabricated on their own terms (caching correctness, fallback-order preservation, lock-serialization/thread-safety observations), but Y edges out with three concrete, cite-and-line-numbered findings (description mismatch, missing docstrings, untested caching behavior with a specific mocking suggestion) versus X's two, giving Y slightly better precision and actionability. Neither includes a LATENT GAP CHECK section.
